title: Access Profiles and Statistics
breadcrumbs: Design Considerations for Performance and Scalability
text: Access Profiles and Statistics The access profile is the sequence of all access paths needed by a user view. In the following figures, an access profile is graphically represented by annotations on the entity boxes: Single arrow—Indicates the direction of access and that only one occurrence of the entity is expected in an access path Double arrow—Indicates the direction of access and that several entity occurrences are expected to be accessed Number in the right-hand corner of the entity box—Indicates the access sequence of the entities, or the depth Examples The following figure shows a simple access profile diagram for the access profile of the retrieval 'Find order 0024 and then find the company it is for.' Figure 1. Access Profile for Single Occurrence The following illustration shows the access profile for 'Find the country USA and then find all companies that reside there': Figure 2. Access Profile with Multiple Occurrences Expected The following illustration shows the access profile where several companies are retrieved and, for each company, the country where the company is located. All the orders for each company must be retrieved. Note that both COUNTRY and ORDER are at level two. Figure 3. Simple Access Profile Consider a more complicated example. In this example, information for a complete configuration needs to be retrieved. The access profile has been marked on the subset of the modeled entities in the following illustration: Figure 4. Complex Access Profile Access profile statistics Access profile statistics show how many occurrences are accessed and how often the relationships are used. For example, the following table shows the statistics for the access profile used for retrieving a complete configuration. Table 1. Access profile statistics table Access Path 1 Via 2 No. of selected occ.'s 3 Access stress value 4 > CONFIGURATION ID_CONFIGURATION 1 1 CONFIGURATION >> PART ID_CONFIGURATION 20 20 PART > DELIVERYLINE ID_DELIVERY, ID_DELIVERYLINE 1 20 DELIVERYLINE >> COVERED_ITEM ID_DELIVERY, ID_DELIVERYLINE 3 60 COVERED_ITEM > ORDERLINE ORDER_NR, ID_ORDERLINE 1 60 ORDERLINE > MATERIAL ID_MATERIAL 1 60 Total Access Stress Rating 5 221 1 Access Path shows the entities involved (including the direction of access). 2 Via shows the selection criteria for the occurrence. 3 No. of selected occurrences indicates the average rate of occurrences expected to be selected using the selection criteria for an individual entity. 4 Access Stress Value indicates the average number of occurrences expected to be selected based on previously retrieved occurrences for an individual entity. 5 Total Access Stress Rating indicates the average number of occurrences expected to be selected for all entities.
